title: stenolobium alatum
intro: Stenolobium alatum, also known as the velvet bean or monkey ladder, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Africa, Asia, and Australia. It is a climber with compound leaves and showy white flowers. The plant contains a compound called L-DOPA, which has been used in traditional medicine to treat various health conditions, including Parkinson's disease and depression, due to its ability to increase dopamine levels in the brain. The dried seeds and leaves of the plant can be made into herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to address these conditions, as well as to reduce inflammation and improve cognitive function.
